snp3.5:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Rājagaha on Vulture Peak Mountain.
snp3.5:1.3: Then the young brahman Māgha went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
snp3.5:1.4: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side.
snp3.5:1.5: As he was sitting there, he said to the Blessed One,
snp3.5:2.1: “Master Gotama, I am generous, a master of giving, magnanimous, responsive to requests.
snp3.5:2.3:
Having searched for wealth righteously, then—with wealth righteously gained, righteously acquired—I give to one,
I give to two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, one hundred, and to more.
snp3.5:2.4: Thus giving, thus sacrificing, do I produce much merit?”
snp3.5:3.1: “Certainly, young man, thus giving, thus sacrificing, you produce much merit.
snp3.5:3.2: Anyone who is generous, a master of giving, magnanimous, responsive to requests,
snp3.5:3.3: who searches for wealth righteously and, having searched for wealth righteously, then—with wealth righteously gained,
snp3.5:3.4:
righteously acquired—gives to one, to two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine,
ten, twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, one hundred, and to more, produces much merit.”
snp3.5:3.5: Then the young brahman Māgha addressed the Blessed One in verse:
snp3.5:4.3: wearing ochre robes, wandering without home:
snp3.5:4.4: When a householder responsive to requests, a master of giving,
snp3.5:4.5: makes a sacrifice in hopes of merit, looking for merit,
snp3.5:4.6: giving food & drink to others here,
snp3.5:4.7:
how is the offering purified for the one making the sacrifice?”
The Buddha:
snp3.5:5.1: “When a householder responsive to requests, a master of giving,
snp3.5:5.3: makes a sacrifice in hopes of merit, looking for merit,
snp3.5:5.4: giving food & drink to others here:
snp3.5:5.5:
Such a person achieves his aim in terms of the recipient.”
Māgha:
snp3.5:6.1: “When a householder responsive to requests, a master of giving,
snp3.5:6.3: makes a sacrifice in hopes of merit, looking for merit,
snp3.5:6.4: giving food & drink to others here:
snp3.5:6.5:
Point out to me the recipient.”
The Buddha:
snp3.5:7.2: wander in the world, having nothing, fully accomplished, their minds restrained:
snp3.5:7.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering,
snp3.5:7.4: to them a brahman aiming at merit should sacrifice.
snp3.5:8.1: Those who have cut all fetters & bonds,
snp3.5:8.2: tamed, released, with no afflictions, no desires:
snp3.5:8.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering,
snp3.5:8.4: to them a brahman aiming at merit should sacrifice.
snp3.5:9.1: Those released from all fetters & bonds,
snp3.5:9.2: tamed, released, with no afflictions, no desires:
snp3.5:9.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:10.2: their holy life fulfilled, their effluents ended:
snp3.5:10.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:11.1: Those in whom no deceptiveness dwells, no conceit,
snp3.5:11.2: their holy life fulfilled, their effluents ended:
snp3.5:11.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:12.1: Those devoid of greed, unpossessive, undesiring,
snp3.5:12.2: their holy life fulfilled, their effluents ended:
snp3.5:12.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:13.1: Those who aren’t prey to cravings,
snp3.5:13.2: who wander without possessiveness, having crossed over the flood:
snp3.5:13.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:15.1: Those who, having abandoned sensuality, wander without home,
snp3.5:15.2: their minds well-restrained, straight as a shuttle:
snp3.5:15.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:16.1: Those devoid of passion, their faculties well-centered,
snp3.5:16.2: released like the moon from the grasp of an eclipse:
snp3.5:16.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:17.1: Having calmed themselves, those devoid of passion, unprovoked,
snp3.5:17.2: who have no (future) destinations, having abandoned them here:
snp3.5:17.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:18.1: Having abandoned birth & death without trace,
snp3.5:18.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:19.1: They who, with themselves as refuge, wander in the world,
snp3.5:19.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:20.1: They who know here, as it really is—
snp3.5:20.2: ‘This is the last birth, there is no further becoming’:
snp3.5:20.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering.
snp3.5:21.1: An attainer-of-knowledge, delighting in jhāna, mindful,
snp3.5:21.2: attained to self-awakening, the refuge of many:
snp3.5:21.3: To him, at the right time, you should bestow an offering,
snp3.5:21.4:
to him a brahman aiming at merit should sacrifice.”
Māgha:
snp3.5:22.1: “Yes, my question was not in vain.
snp3.5:22.3: You, Blessed One, have pointed out the recipient.
snp3.5:22.4: You know this here, as it really is,
snp3.5:22.5: because this Dhamma has been experienced by you.
snp3.5:23.1: When a householder responsive to requests, a master of giving,
snp3.5:23.2: makes a sacrifice in hopes of merit, looking for merit,
snp3.5:23.3: giving food & drink to others here:
snp3.5:23.4: Point out to me, Blessed One, the consummation of the sacrifice.”
snp3.5:24.1:
The Buddha:
“Sacrifice, and while sacrificing, Māgha,
snp3.5:24.4:
make your mind clear everywhere.
For one sacrificing, the sacrifice is the object,
snp3.5:25.1: Taking a stand there, he abandons faults.
snp3.5:25.2: He—devoid of passion, subduing aversion, developing a mind of goodwill
snp3.5:25.4: day & night, continually heedful— should pervade all the directions
snp3.5:26.3: By means of what mind does one go to the Brahmā world?
snp3.5:26.4: Say this, sage, when asked by me who doesn’t know.
snp3.5:26.5: As the Blessed One is my witness,
snp3.5:26.6: I have seen Brahmā today, for you are equal to Brahmā for us—it’s true!
snp3.5:27.1: How, shining one, does one reappear in the Brahmā world?”
snp3.5:27.4: with the threefold sacrifice consummation, such a one would achieve his aim
snp3.5:28.2:
one responsive to requests reappears, I say, in the Brahmā world.”
When this was said, the young brahman Māgha said to the Blessed One:
“Magnificent, Master Gotama!
Magnificent!
Just as if he were to place upright what was overturned, to reveal what was hidden, to show the way to one who was lost,
snp3.5:28.3:
or to carry a lamp into the dark so that those with eyes could see forms,
in the same way has Master Gotama—through many lines of reasoning—made the Dhamma clear.
I go to Master Gotama for refuge, to the Dhamma, & to the Saṅgha of monks.
May Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from this day forward, for life.”
